Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration Or Alternatively Transfer Venue
Off Calendar Sep-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Georgia Z. Schneider of the Law Offices of Sean Ponist, P.S. (415) 798-2222, appears as counsel for plaintiffs/Opposing parties, John K. Baldwin, ET AL.

Attorney, Scott Berman of Pierce & Shearer LLP (650) 843-1900, appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties, Cardinal Parcel Service Inc., ET AL.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS CARDINAL PARCEL SERVICE INC., BRUIN PARCEL SERVICE INC., DIABLO PARCEL SERVICE INC., PACIFIC PARCEL SERVICE INC. , WATCHTOVER CAPITAL MANAGEMENT LLC, ALLAN MENDELL AND ALEX BERINGER'S Motion To Compel Arbitration Or Alternatively Transfer Venue is GRANTED and this case is stayed pending the completion of the arbitration proceedings. The Federal Arbitration Act governs because the Purchase Agreement involves interstate commerce. Plaintiffs' defenses of fraud and illegality must be determined by the arbitrator since plaintiffs challenge the Purchase Agreement, not the arbitration clause. Case law construing the FAA requires that the arbitrator determine the issue of the contract's validity unless there is a challenge to the arbitration clause itself. (Buckeye Check Cashing, Inc. v. Cardegna (2006) 546 U.S. 440, 445-46.) Plaintiffs have not shown that the arbitration clause is either procedurally or substantively unconscionable. Defendants' alternative motion to transfer venue is denied. The Purchase Agreement does not contain an enforceable venue-selection clause. Venue is proper in San Francisco County per CCP 395.5. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR #5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
